$comment: Canonical definitions of the six invariants of the Warp Commerce Model plus the money_breakdown_sum structural rule and the loyalty-liability (Invariant 1, fourth clause) rule. Names and fix hints are the single source for every language binding's runtime checkers, so the human-readable violation messages agree across languages.

I-1.name: Value Conservation
I-1.description: Value is neither created nor destroyed by a transaction; it is transferred. A single commitment must not mix currencies in its subject without an explicit conversion.
I-1.fix: Convert all monetary values to one currency with convert(), or record an explicit CurrencyConversion in the terms.

I-2.name: State Monotonicity
I-2.description: State machines only advance along the legal transition edges; a terminal state never moves backward. History timestamps never decrease.
I-2.fix: Only the model's valid transitions are allowed; a reversal must be a new Commitment with parties exchanged.

I-3.name: Capacity Verification
I-3.description: A party must have the verified capacity for a role before a commitment depending on that capacity is Accepted (a buyer must have can_buy).
I-3.fix: Verify party capacity (can_buy) before transitioning to Accepted.

I-4.name: Temporal Integrity
I-4.description: Commitments form before Fulfillments execute. A fulfillment cannot start before its commitment reached Accepted.
I-4.fix: Commitments form before Fulfillments execute - accept the commitment first.

I-5.name: Identity Permanence
I-5.description: Every identifier is globally unique and never reused across the lifetime of the system.
I-5.fix: IDs are globally unique and never reused; generate a fresh id.

I-6.name: Commitment Tree Consistency
I-6.description: A parent commitment's value equals the sum of its children's values (in one base currency), within minor-unit tolerance.
I-6.fix: Child commitment values must sum to the parent; recalculate after any substitution or cancellation (use allocate() for exact splits).

money_breakdown_sum.doc: Structural rule for the MoneyBreakdown type: the component amounts must sum to the stated total, compared with the currency's minor-unit tolerance (half the smallest minor unit), not exact float equality. Discount components carry a negative amount. All components and the total must share one currency.

loyalty_liability.doc: Invariant 1, fourth clause. Loyalty points / merchant currency are the only ValueForm where value CREATION is the primary operation. Conservation applies to the issuer's total outstanding liability pool: outstanding_points * redemption_value_per_point must not exceed the issuer's capacity to honor them. Liability and capacity must share one currency.
